bead.title: Reject ancestor dependency edges in bead create and dep add
bead.description:
PROBLEM
`ddx bead dep add` and the bead create path still allow dependency edges that point back into the parent chain. That lets a child re-introduce its parent as a dep and creates the deadlock described in the parent bead.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go:520-680` validates existence, self-deps, and cycles, but it does not reject an edge when the target bead is the caller's parent or any ancestor in the parent chain. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328-378` also lacks a `--depends-on` create input path, so create-time validation has to cover both the CLI flag and the shared store guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Add ancestry-aware validation in the bead store and thread a `--depends-on` create flag through `cli/cmd/bead.go` so `ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y` hard-rejects when `X == Y` or `X` is any ancestor of `Y`. The error shape must name the offending ancestor and print the parent chain.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decomposition path, doctor repair logic, work-loop remediation, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go
- cli/cmd/bead.go
- cli/cmd/bead_test.go (new or existing create-path test coverage)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
bead.acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDepAddRejectsParentAncestor` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor` passes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
4. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The requested ancestor-validation behavior is already present in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/cmd/bead.go`; the remaining work is to rerun the broad sweep on a less contended runner or after the host load settles.
